title: Asclepias fallax
intro: Asclepias fallax, also known as the fall axillarias or fall milkwort, is a species of milkweed plant native to the southwestern United States. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Bioactive constituents of Asclepias fallax include cardenolides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in the form of a cough syrup or expectorant to relieve respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Asclepias fallax is classified within the family Apocynaceae, specifically within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. This subfamily is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the milkweed family. The genus Asclepias is native to North America, and Asclepias fallax is one of the species within this genus. The plant is closely related to other milkweed species, which are known for their distinctive flowers and importance in the ecosystem. Asclepias fallax is often used as a reference in the study of the evolution of milkweed species.
